overview: The Europeana webpage underwent significant changes between March 2020 and September 2022, shifting from a focus on Women's History Month to a broader exploration of European cultural heritage, and simplifying its navigation structure.

ai.overview: The Europeana webpage underwent significant changes between March 2020 and September 2022, shifting from a focus on Women's History Month to a broader exploration of European cultural heritage, and simplifying its navigation structure.

snapshots.fullText: Welcome to Europeana Skip to page contents Search Collections Teachers About us You're viewing the new Europeana experience. Go to the original Europeana. Welcome to Europeana Discover inspiring cultural heritage from European museums, galleries, libraries and archives Retrat d'estudi, Ajuntament de Girona Public Domain Women's History Month Join our celebration of remarkable women throughout March Pioneers Discover the lives and achievements of eight remarkable historical European women. Women at Work Women's work has long been overlooked, raising questions of equality, rights and work-life balance. Suffragettes The long and hard battle for social reform and a voice for women in politics. Aletta Jacobs Aletta Jacobs was one of the women involved in fighting for suffrage who had a profound impact on Dutch society. Silhouettes: a century of female fashion Discover the changing silhouettes of women's fashion in the 20th century. Maria Sibylla Merian Maria Sibylla Merian (1647–1717) was a naturalist and scientific illustrator, and one of the first naturalists to observe insects directly. Madame de Staël Madame de Staël was a politically engaged woman of letters, who survived the French Revolution and was exiled more than once by Napoleon. Women in Culture and Tech: Mariya Gabriel Commissioner Gabriel shares her vision for women in today’s digital world with Europeana. Explore more women's history Galleries Exhibitions Blogs Celebrity chefs of the past Meet the women who taught Europe to cook. Anna May Wong Challenging the stereotypes of Hollywood Countess Markievicz, Irish revolutionary Constance Markievicz was a leader in the fight for Ireland’s independence and a political pioneer. Photography pioneer: Anna Atkins’ algae cyanotypes British botanist and photographer, Anna Atkins was a pioneer in photography. Show more blogs Our mission Europeana empowers the cultural heritage sector in its digital transformation. We develop expertise, tools and policies to embrace digital change and encourage partnerships that foster innovation. Find us elsewhere (opens in new window) (opens in new window) (opens in new window) (opens in new window) More info About Europeana Meet the team For developers Become a data partner Help Help Terms of Use and Privacy Policy Customise website language English Български Čeština Dansk Deutsch Ελληνικά Español Eesti Euskara Suomi Français Gaeilge Hrvatski Magyar Italiano Lietuvių Latviešu Malti Nederlands Polski Português Română Slovenčina Slovenščina Svenska Europeana is an initiative of the European Union, financed by the European Union’s Connecting Europe Facility and European Union Member States. The Europeana services, including this website, are operated by a consortium led by the Europeana Foundation under a service contract with the European Commission. The European Commission does not guarantee the accuracy of the information and accepts no responsibility or liability whatsoever with regard to the information on this website. Neither the European Commission, nor any person acting on the European Commission’s behalf, is responsible or liable for the accuracy or use of the information on this website. This website uses cookies to ensure you get the best experience on our website. By clicking or navigating the site, you agree to allow our collection of information through cookies. More info

sections.heading: Women's History Month

snapshots.fullText: Welcome to Europeana Skip to page contents Search Collections Teachers About us You're viewing the new Europeana experience. Go to the original Europeana. Welcome to Europeana Discover inspiring cultural heritage from European museums, galleries, libraries and archives Retrat d'estudi, Foto Lux, Ajuntament de Girona Public Domain Women's History Month Join our celebration of remarkable women throughout March Exhibition Pioneers Discover the lives and achievements of eight remarkable historical European women. Gallery Women at Work Women's work has long been overlooked, raising questions of equality, rights and work-life balance. Gallery Suffragettes The long and hard battle for social reform and a voice for women in politics. Exhibition Aletta Jacobs Aletta Jacobs was one of the women involved in fighting for suffrage who had a profound impact on Dutch society. Gallery Silhouettes: a century of female fashion Discover the changing silhouettes of women's fashion in the 20th century. Gallery Maria Sibylla Merian Maria Sibylla Merian (1647–1717) was a naturalist and scientific illustrator, and one of the first naturalists to observe insects directly. Exhibition Madame de Staël Madame de Staël was a politically engaged woman of letters, who survived the French Revolution and was exiled more than once by Napoleon. Women in Culture and Tech: Mariya Gabriel Commissioner Gabriel shares her vision for women in today’s digital world with Europeana. Explore more women's history Galleries Exhibitions Blogs Blog Celebrity chefs of the past Meet the women who taught Europe to cook. Blog Anna May Wong Challenging the stereotypes of Hollywood Blog Countess Markievicz, Irish revolutionary Constance Markievicz was a leader in the fight for Ireland’s independence and a political pioneer. Blog Photography pioneer: Anna Atkins’ algae cyanotypes British botanist and photographer, Anna Atkins was a pioneer in photography. Show more blogs Our mission Europeana empowers the cultural heritage sector in its digital transformation. We develop expertise, tools and policies to embrace digital change and encourage partnerships that foster innovation. The European Commission does not guarantee the accuracy of the information and accepts no responsibility or liability whatsoever with regard to the information on this website. Welcome to Europeana Discover inspiring cultural heritage from European museums, galleries, libraries and archives Praterallee im Herbst, Olga Wisinger-Florian, Österreichische Galerie Belvedere CC BY-SA What's new GIF IT UP 2020 Your favourite annual GIF-making competition is back! (opens in new window) Discovering Europe Discover beautiful and historical places throughout Europe Exhibition It was 30 years ago The European Parliament, the fall of the Berlin Wall and German reunification Blog Rewards of the races Running and European athletes Blog Meet the Great Belzoni Meet Italian Indiana Jones and other Egyptology pioneers Blog Jenny Lind: the Swedish Nightingale Hugely talented Swedish opera singer who enjoyed worldwide fame in the mid-19th century Gallery Maps of Europe How cartographers and others have defined Europe, its lands and borders through maps Subscribe to our Newsletter Get the latest info about European digital cultural heritage with Europeana's Newsletter (opens in new window) Explore editorial on Europeana Blogs Read stories from across Europe illustrated by cultural heritage collections (opens in new window) Galleries Browse our galleries to discover a large variety of collections from around Europe Exhibitions Enjoy our exhibitions: long-reads on European cultural heritage Topics Explore the wide range of topics and themes to be found on Europeana

snapshots.fullText: Discover inspiring European cultural heritage | Europeana Skip to page contents Home Collections Stories Log in / Join Home Collections Stories Teachers About us Help Log in / Join You're viewing the new Europeana experience. Go to the original Europeana. Europeana Sport This summer, explore the heritage of sport across Europe EXPLORE What's new Blog post The Tailteann Games Irish sporting competitions in the 1920s and 1930s Blog post Cancelled Olympics Exploring the Olympic Games that never happened Blog post Podcast: Century of Change Listen to a podcast on the stories of Nobel Prize winners throughout the 20th Century Blog post Irish emigration Exploring stories and oral histories relating to Irish emigration in the 20th century Blog post Roma slavery in Romania A history of more than 500 years of slavery of Roma population on the territory of Romania Gallery Art and sport Explore this gallery showing how artists have been influenced by and portrayed sports and games Gallery Surfing and windsurfing Explore images and films of surfing and windsurfing from cultural heritage collections across Europe. Blog post Hajj and the story of the Mahmal Discover the longstanding tradition of offering intricately produced textiles to the holy shrines. Galleries Vaccines in history The long view of vaccination and immunology Still life autochromes The autochrome is an early form of colour photography, resulting in vivid colours with an almost painterly quality Kendo Kendo-related material from cultural heritage institutions across Europe Create a gallery Save your favourite items and create your own galleries Get involved Take part in our projects and enjoy our puzzles and games Create an account Save your favourite items and create your own galleries Share your stories We'd love to hear your stories of migration, sport, working life or World War I Transcribathon Decipher, transcribe and annotate handwritten stories from Europeana [phone](opens in new window) Colouring Books Find your crayons! Sharpen your coloured pencils! And enjoy adding colours in one of the books we have prepared for you.
